title: Vegan German Eggs Quiche
description: A rich, crumbly, vegan quiche that's perfect for a lazy Sunday morning. Inspired by traditional German cuisine, this dish features tofu scramble and smoked tofu instead of eggs.

ingredients.name: non-dairy milk
ingredients.notes: such as almond, soy, or coconut milk

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the chilled dough on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Transfer the dough to a 9-inch round baking dish and trim the edges.
In a skillet, sauté the crumbled tofu, smoked tofu, and shio-cured tofu until lightly browned, breaking up the tofu with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the nutritional yeast, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Pour the non-dairy milk into the skillet and stir to combine, allowing the mixture to simmer for about 5 minutes, or until the liquid has thickened slightly.
Add the shio-cured capers and turmeric (if using) to the skillet and stir to combine.
Pour the tofu mixture into the dough-lined baking dish and spread it evenly.
Garnish the quiche with chopped parsley and bake for 35-40 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is set.
Let the quiche cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
